mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies an economic slowdown, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) alongside rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion (high GDPC1 growth), robust housing starts (HOUST) signal strong investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive short-term market sentiment and equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 2.5%, HOUST month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and overall supply-side weakness, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of instances where INDPRO 3-month annualized growth falls below -2%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive short-term equity market sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary environment, which reduces the cost of capital and boosts corporate valuations, leading to positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth < 2% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS has not increased over the past three months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery (declining UNRATE, rising GDPC1), increasing housing starts (HOUST) signal a strong rebound in investment and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns as the economy normalizes.
failure_condition: In periods identified as post-recession recovery (e.g., UNRATE declining from peak, GDPC1 growing > 2% annually), HOUST month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 20-day forward returns.
